summary: ICDex grid trading bot that places buy/sell orders using ckUSDT on the ICDex exchange. The bot loop runs correctly (fetching prices, cancelling orders) but is not placing new buy/sell orders after cancellation.
architectural_notes: Bot runs a trading loop every ~10 seconds: fetches best bid/ask prices from ICDex, cancels open orders, then should place new grid orders
Bug: after 'Successfully cancelled all open orders', bot does not proceed to place buy/sell orders - loop restarts without placing orders
known_issues: Bot does not place buy/sell orders after cancelling open orders - 'Placing buy/sell order' messages never appear in the activity log

new_feature_requests.summary: Investigate and fix the backend trading loop so that after successfully cancelling open orders, the bot proceeds to calculate grid levels and place new buy and sell orders on ICDex. The activity log must show 'Placing buy order' and 'Placing sell order' messages following each 'Successfully cancelled all open orders' entry.

Add detailed activity log entries throughout the order placement phase so that each step after cancellation is traceable: e.g., 'Calculating grid levels', 'Placing buy order at price X', 'Placing sell order at price X', 'Successfully placed N buy orders and M sell orders', and any error encountered during placement.
